mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month change in INDPRO signals improving real production and operating leverage, raising expected earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO level was 119.678 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO changes do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 6-month changes in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of FEDFUNDS above its trailing 12-month average indicates an active tightening impulse that raises discount rates and tightens credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS level was 3.5 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates over 3 months while FEDFUNDS also rises over 3 months, inflation pressure forces policy restraint and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL level was 196.1 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-up state has a nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 forward return, or if an additive single-series model fully eliminates the interaction term at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals broad labor-market strengthening that supports household income and revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE level was 4.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not outperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 20 basis points, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and future construction-linked spending, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST level was 2042.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST drawdowns produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if their mean return is not below the full-sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in RSAFS signals strengthening consumer spending momentum, raising near-term sales and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS level was 350106.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS changes fail to produce a positive average 5-day S&P 500 return, or if the 5-day directional hit rate is not above 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while CPIAUCSL rises, stagflation pressure reduces real earnings growth and prevents valuation relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth declines and CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 level was 11088.6 as of t-5mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-slowing and CPIAUCSL-rising state does not have lower 60-day returns than periods with GDPC1 acceleration, or if the signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of UNRATE above its trailing 12-month average signals labor-market deterioration that lowers expected consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE level was 4.9 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE upward-crossing events produce nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if their downside hit rate is below 52% in walk-forward tests.
